Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Destiny Pham's loan file in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to Destiny Pham or mortgage_los_search_borrowers with query set to Destiny Pham. Agent discovers Destiny Pham as a borrower (los_borrower_d679dee523c0, destiny.pham@gmail.com) and retrieves her associated loan file(s). Note: there may be multiple Destiny entries in the system (Destiny Pham vs Destiny Huang), creating a disambiguation trap.
OE 2: Search emails for Destiny Pham's prepayment penalty complaint using search_emails with query containing Destiny Pham prepayment or prepayment penalty (or similar). Agent discovers Destiny's email to Jasmine Brooks saying she found a prepayment penalty in her closed loan documents, that Keisha told her there was no penalty, that English is not her first language, and that she feels things were not explained clearly.
OE 3: Search emails for the 1-star Google review from Destiny Pham using search_emails with query containing Destiny Pham review or review discrimination (or similar). Agent discovers the Google review notification stating Destiny had a terrible experience with Keystone and specifically Keisha Williams, alleging poor communication, delayed callbacks, denial, and feeling treated differently than other applicants in a discriminatory way.
OE 4: Search emails for the Aisha Walker CFPB complaint using search_emails with query containing Aisha or CFPB or federal complaint (or similar). Agent discovers the CFPB complaint from consumerresponse@cfpb.gov addressed to Denise Holloway, alleging discrimination against Aisha Walker (a Black applicant denied for insufficient income) while a similarly situated white applicant named James Goldstein received approval. The complaint names Keisha Williams as the loan officer who was dismissive and failed to clearly explain the denial basis. The company must respond within 15 calendar days.
OE 5: Look up Aisha Walker's loan in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to Aisha Walker or LN-2024-00044. Agent retrieves the loan details for Aisha Walker's denied application to understand the underwriting outcome and file history.
OE 6: Look up James Goldstein's loan in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to James Goldstein. Agent retrieves the comparator borrower's approved loan to compare against Aisha Walker's denial as referenced in the CFPB complaint.
OE 7: Search emails for Denise's investigation plan and CFPB response coordination using search_emails with query containing CFPB response or investigation plan or fair lending review (or similar). Agent discovers Denise's email to Grace and Robert outlining the formal fair lending review plan, requesting ops to pull both loan files for side-by-side comparison on DTI, credit profile, income documentation, condition history, and decision rationale. Agent also finds Denise requesting Keisha's written chronology and underwriting rationale for both files.
OE 8: Search emails for Denise contacting outside counsel about the CFPB response using search_emails with query containing fair lending counsel or Megan or outside counsel (or similar). Agent discovers Denise's email to outside fair lending counsel Laura requesting guidance on framing the response and acknowledging documentation gaps.
OE 9: Search emails for Grace's internal investigation order on Destiny Pham using search_emails with query containing Destiny Pham timeline or file timeline Destiny (or similar). Agent discovers Grace's email to the team ordering a clean internal timeline on Destiny Pham's denied file, asking Keisha for her timeline first, Jasmine to review contact history, and Denise to flag any fair lending or privacy concerns.
OE 10: Search emails for Jasmine Brooks' CRM engagement gap analysis on Destiny Pham using search_emails with query containing Jasmine CRM engagement or response-time gaps (or similar). Agent discovers Jasmine's email reporting that she pulled Destiny Pham's CRM engagement history and found response-time gaps worth validating, noting that some inbound borrower touches do not show a prompt callback or documented follow-up in CRM.
OE 11: Search emails for Keisha Williams' timeline response on Destiny Pham using search_emails with query containing Keisha timeline Destiny or Keisha recollection (or similar). Agent discovers Keisha's email to Grace admitting she missed at least one callback window and had a couple slower replies while juggling closings, that the file was tight on qualifying from the start, and that she did not use any language related to race or any protected class.
OE 12: Search emails for Denise's 3 audit findings on Keisha's files using search_emails with query containing audit findings Keisha or spot check Keisha or compliance audit (or similar). Agent discovers the audit findings including adverse action timing not documented cleanly on the Catherine Pham file, missing changed circumstance evidence before a revised LE on the Catherine Adeyemi file, and a third finding about a bank statement condition cleared prematurely.
OE 13: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing Keisha audit or compliance audit findings or spot check (or similar). Agent discovers Denise's Slack post detailing the 3 findings on Keisha Williams' loans from the spot check and requesting written responses by end of week.
OE 14: Search emails for Keisha's formal dispute of the audit findings using search_emails with query containing formal response audit or dispute audit findings or Keisha disagree (or similar). Agent discovers Keisha's email to Denise formally disputing Findings 1 and 2 while accepting Finding 3. Keisha disagrees that the Catherine Pham adverse action timing was late and disputes the Catherine Adeyemi changed circumstance finding. Agent also discovers Denise's reply maintaining her position based on file process and documentation standards.
OE 15: Search emails for Keisha's whistleblower retaliation complaint using search_emails with query containing retaliation or whistleblower or commission complaint (or similar). Agent discovers Keisha's formal retaliation complaint to Robert alleging her commission reduction under the 2026 LO comp grid was imposed in retaliation for her written dispute of Denise's audit findings, requesting preservation of all related records. Agent also finds Keisha's email to outside counsel Megan seeking representation.
OE 16: Search emails for Grace's communication lockdown order related to the Destiny Pham Google review using search_emails with query containing do not reply or do not engage or review response or social media (or similar). Agent discovers Grace's email instructing the team not to reply, comment, or engage with the Destiny Pham review thread from company or personal accounts, and to route any media or partner questions to Grace directly.
OE 17: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing Destiny or Destiny Pham or Aisha or fair lending (or similar). Agent discovers Slack messages about Destiny's complaint being logged, discussions about the Google review response, and any compliance-related threads about Keisha's borrowers.
OE 18: Search CRM engagements related to Destiny Pham and Aisha Walker using CRM tools such as crm_list_engagements or crm_show_data. Agent discovers CRM engagement entries tracking the CFPB complaint on Aisha Walker (15-day clock started, LO chronology requested), and Destiny Pham entries related to the appraisal, term change, and lock extension discussions.
OE 19: Look up Grace Yamamoto's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query Grace or Yamamoto. Agent confirms grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com for the email.
OE 20: Send Grace an email from Brittany using send_email with sender brittany.wallace@keystonemortgage.com, recipients grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject referencing Destiny Pham walk-in and compliance concerns (or similar), and content explaining that Destiny walked into the office, what she reported about the prepayment penalty and Keisha's representation, that she mentioned another borrower named Aisha who filed a federal complaint about the same LO, what the agent found about the CFPB complaint and its status, the existing internal investigations (Grace's timeline order, Denise's fair lending review, Jasmine's CRM analysis), the audit findings on Keisha's files and her formal dispute, and any pattern indicators across multiple borrowers involving Keisha Williams.
OE 21: Add a compliance note to Destiny Pham's loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan_id for Destiny's loan, action set to compliance_alert (or similar valid action type), author set to Brittany Wallace, and detail documenting Destiny's walk-in visit, her report about the prepayment penalty discrepancy, her mention of another borrower Aisha with a federal complaint, and that Grace and Denise have been notified.
OE 22: Post an update to the compliance channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id C004 (the compliance-alerts channel) and payload summarizing that Destiny Pham walked into the office reporting a prepayment penalty she says Keisha told her did not exist, that she mentioned another borrower named Aisha who filed a federal complaint about the same loan officer, and that a full investigation summary has been sent to Grace.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
